behaviors.description: A request whose signature does not match its body is rejected with a 401 response; the body is not forwarded.
behaviors.justification: Rationale A (failure boundary): this checks handler-to-queue handoff suppression on auth failure, not just pure HMAC math. Rationale B (mock-hide): unit-testing only `verifySignature()` can pass while middleware/body parsing + handler wiring still forwards invalid events. Integration-brake: no real OS/network/subprocess requirement in behavior text, no AI dependency, no multi-agent chain. Rejected higher level: no wording requires real network timing or external process behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Run the HTTP handler in-process with real request parsing middleware and a fake/in-memory queue adapter; send a tampered body/signature pair; assert `401` and assert queue `enqueue` is not called.

behaviors.description: An event is written to the persistent queue before the HTTP 200 acknowledgment is returned to the sender.
behaviors.justification: Rationale A: this is an ordering contract across modules (HTTP handler and queue write). Rationale B: a unit test on response code alone misses race/order bugs where `200` is sent before durable write resolves. Integration-brake: no required real OS primitive or external network call is stated; AI not involved. Rejected System: behavior text requires write-before-ack ordering, not real internet/subprocess behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Use in-process HTTP request + real queue module (or local test persistence) instrumented with timestamps/hooks; assert queue write completion occurs before response completion.

behaviors.description: The handler returns the HTTP response without waiting for downstream processing of the event to finish.
behaviors.justification: Rationale A: verifies decoupling between request path and async processor module. Rationale B: mocking away async boundaries can hide accidental `await` on downstream work. Integration-brake: no explicit need for real OS/network/subprocess; AI not involved. Rejected System: behavior is about module-level async orchestration, not external infra semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke handler through in-process HTTP with downstream processor returning a controllable delayed promise; assert response is sent before that promise resolves.

behaviors.description: The response arrives within 3 seconds wall-clock time even when downstream processing of earlier events is slow and concurrent.
behaviors.justification: Rationale A: explicit wall-clock SLA under concurrency is an event-loop/runtime behavior boundary. Rationale B: mocked timers or single-threaded unit harness can miss real scheduling contention and socket/request handling delays. REAL-INFRA applies: real clock + concurrent request handling are required to catch this failure mode. Rejected lower Integration: behavior text explicitly anchors to wall-clock under concurrent slow work, which is where in-process fakes can under-represent latency.
behaviors.test_description: Start the real service process, generate concurrent load with slow downstream jobs already in flight, send fresh webhook requests over real HTTP, and assert end-to-end response latency stays <3s.

behaviors.description: An event whose ID was already received is not written to the queue a second time.
behaviors.justification: Rationale A: dedupe correctness is at handler/store boundary (state handoff across requests), not a single pure function only. Rationale B: mocking persistence can hide uniqueness/state bugs (e.g., transaction timing, stale reads). Integration-brake: no explicit real network/subprocess requirement; no AI dependency. Rejected System: behavior text does not require OS-level or external-service behavior beyond local persistence semantics.
behaviors.test_description: With real dedupe path (handler + persistent queue/repo in test env), submit same event ID twice and assert exactly one persisted enqueue record.

behaviors.description: When the queue reports it is full, the handler returns 503 with a Retry-After header.
behaviors.justification: Rationale A: this is translation of queue backpressure signal into HTTP semantics across module boundary. Rationale B: unit-testing status mapping without actual handler-queue interaction can miss miswired error propagation. Integration-brake: no real OS/network/subprocess needed by wording; no AI. Rejected System: no requirement for real infrastructure effects, only correct boundary mapping.
behaviors.test_description: Wire handler to queue implementation/stub that returns a `QueueFull` condition; assert HTTP `503` and `Retry-After` header are present and body is not enqueued.

behaviors.description: The persistent queue actually enforces its configured capacity limit and rejects events that would exceed it.
behaviors.justification: Rationale A: this validates queue logic plus persistence/config wiring, beyond a single function. Rationale B: mocking storage would likely always pass and miss real capacity enforcement defects in repository interactions. Integration-brake: behavior does not explicitly require external network/subprocess/OS primitives beyond local persistence; no AI; no multi-agent chain. Rejected System: no concrete text requiring real external infra behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate the real persistent queue against local test storage with capacity N, enqueue N events successfully, then assert enqueue N+1 is rejected with the expected capacity error.
